claim: Claude Code hooks (pre-tool-use, post-tool-use, notification, stop, sub-agent-stop) provide surgical observability and control over agentic coding workflows, enabling blocking of dangerous commands like rm -rf before execution
actionable: Configure pre-tool-use hooks in your Claude Code settings.json to block specific tool patterns (e.g., rm, environment file access) by implementing matcher logic that validates tool names and inputs before allowing execution
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Post-tool-use and stop hooks are essential for observability in agentic systems; the stop hook captures the complete chat transcript which is critical for measuring and improving agent performance
actionable: Implement stop hooks that log the full transcript and any tool outputs to JSON files; use post-tool-use hooks to record every tool execution with input/output pairs for analysis
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: UV single-file Python scripts provide powerful isolation for hooks; they can run without external dependencies and cleanly encapsulate logic for pre-tool, post-tool, notification, stop, and sub-agent-stop events
actionable: Write isolated Python scripts in your hooks directory using UV's single-file script format; specify dependencies at the top and run via 'uv run' to minimize external configuration
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: The big three of AI coding (context, model, prompt) remain fundamental principles regardless of tooling; mastering these elements ensures adaptability across Claude Code, Cursor, and future agentic tools
actionable: When evaluating or building agentic systems, always assess context management (what data the agent sees), model selection (reasoning vs speed tradeoffs), and prompt engineering (clarity and structure of instructions)
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Parallel sub-agents in Claude Code can execute multiple tasks simultaneously and report completion via hooks with text-to-speech notifications, enabling async workflows where you can step away while agents work
actionable: Use custom slash commands that spawn parallel sub-agents with the /parallel_sub_agents directive; hook into sub-agent-stop events and use text-to-speech APIs (11Labs, OpenAI, or macOS 'say' command) for notifications
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Claude Code is a new engineering primitive because it combines custom slash commands, hooks, and programmable interaction; this makes it the first programmable agentic coding tool
actionable: Treat Claude Code not just as a code editor but as a foundational primitive you can build other tools on top of; leverage hooks and custom commands to create specialized workflows for your domain
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: Notification hooks can integrate with third-party services (Slack, email, mobile apps) to communicate agent status; this allows integration of agentic workflows into existing team communication systems
actionable: In your notification hook, parse the input JSON and call external APIs (Slack webhooks, ntfy.sh, Telegram, etc.) to send alerts about agent completion or permission requests
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding

claim: By analyzing tool input/output logs from hooks, you can understand what Claude Code is doing at scale and improve prompts by seeing which tools fail or which queries need refinement
actionable: Store all hook outputs (pre-tool, post-tool) in JSON files; periodically analyze failures and errors to identify patterns; use these patterns to refine your system prompts and task descriptions
source_title: I'm HOOKED on Claude Code Hooks: Advanced Agentic Coding
